title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: A do-or-die battle for major fronts, their skippers
content: Having been in the political wilderness for a decade, the UDF  cannot afford to sit out of power for another five years. For the LDF, which suffered a series of setbacks in the Assembly bypolls, the Lok Sabha elections, and the local body polls, the April 9 Assembly elections will be a test of its credentials.  For the National Democratic Alliance too the polls will be decisive, having seen a slump in their vote share in the local body elections

title: ‘New’ Bangladesh-India ties will avoid ‘mistakes of the past’: Foreign Affairs Adviser Humayun Kobir
content: The Foreign Affairs Adviser to Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman says the two sides should do more frequent exchanges at the level of political parties and officials to deal with ‘difficult issues’ so that misunderstandings are avoided.

title: Watch: Ladakh statehood talks resume: What is MHA offering?
content: Talks between Ladakh’s civil society groups and the Union Home Ministry resumed in New Delhi after last month’s deadly protests demanding Statehood. Officials hinted at possible safeguards under Article 371, but the Leh Apex Body and Kargil Democratic Alliance reiterated their demand for inclusion under the Sixth Schedule. The groups also sought the release of activist Sonam Wangchuk. With tensions high since Ladakh lost its special status in 2019, the region awaits clarity on its political future and autonomy.

title: ‘How can Bengal election be fair when so many genuine voters are left out,’ asks Shashi Panja

title: Watch: TMC vs BJP — but the real story is missing voters | West Bengal 2026
content: West Bengal is less than three weeks away from the 2026 Assembly Elections, but the headlines aren’t about rallies or manifestos. They are about the 91 lakh names that have been wiped off the electoral rolls. With the results set for May 4, West Bengal is standing at a crossroads. Is this a necessary “cleanup” of the rolls, or a systemic disenfranchisement?

content: The two Pakistani terrorists in custody are categorised 'A+' and were involved in creating the LeT network in Jammu and Kashmir and other states.

content: According to sources, the accused had previously conducted a reconnaissance of the bank and was aware that the crowd inside is sparse during the afternoon hours and that no security guard is present.

title: AI Cameras, Poll Cancellation For Booth Capturing Among Poll Body's Plans For Bengal
content: AI-enabled CCTV cameras will monitor polling stations in real time. If cameras are switched off or any suspicious activity is detected, repolling can be ordered immediately.

title: Opinion: Pak's 'Global Power' Act Is Fooling No One - Least Of All Its Gulf 'Creditors'
content: Foreign policy is not about short-term optics or daily narratives; it must be assessed over the long term. Pakistan's repeated attempts to project itself as a decisive global player must be seen in this context.

content: The proposed draft amendments are still under consultation, with the deadline for stakeholder feedback now extended.

title: Madhya Pradesh Police Recruits Begin Day With Shlokas. Top Cop Defends Move
content: Article 28(1) of the Indian Constitution is unambiguous: no religious instruction shall be provided in any educational institution wholly maintained out of state funds. Police training schools, funded and run by the state, fall squarely within this framework.

title: AI replacing radiologists: Docs slam Nvidia, Anthropic CEOs for false info
content: America's largest public hospital system CEO is open to replacing radiologists with AI, citing potential for "major savings" in areas like breast cancer screening. This statement follows similar claims from AI leaders, but a neuroradiologist criticizes these views as "fiction" and "ignorance," arguing AI's current impact on radiology is minimal and questioning the motives behind such pronouncements.

title: 'A literal demon': Fans stunned as Sooryavanshi takes on Jasprit Bumrah
content: In an 11-over-a-side contest, Sooryavanshi attacked Bumrah in the second over of the innings. The teenage batter hit two sixes in four deliveries, including one off the first ball he faced, putting Mumbai Indians under pressure early.

title: Middle East turmoil puts India’s trade, energy flows at risk: Report
content: India faces significant external shocks from the Middle East crisis, impacting energy markets, trade, and supply chains. Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are increasing costs for exporters and importers, with sectors like agriculture and labour-intensive industries particularly vulnerable. Higher energy prices and potential slowdowns in Gulf economies could lead to sustained inflation and slower growth for India.

title: Canada has changed immigration rules; changing visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting from April 2026 with passport and citizenship fee increases, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain increased autonomy in immigration assessments, while rural employers benefit from expanded foreign worker quotas.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 20: Ranveer Singh film surges past Rs. 1,025 crore
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has scripted history as the first Hindi film to breach the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Globally, the thriller has amassed Rs. 1,622.72 crore, outperforming blockbusters like Jawan. Despite the IPL 2026 season and a 229-minute runtime, the film remains a powerhouse. To boost family viewership, makers have released updated prints with muted abuses and technical fixes. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs. 1,700 crore mark.

title: No fear: Sooryavanshi smashes Bumrah for six first ball - Watch
content: In the match between Rajasthan Royals (RR) and Mumbai Indians (MI), the young batting prodigy took on Bumrah in the second over of the innings, hitting two sixes in the space of four deliveries. The game had already been shortened to 11 overs per side due to rain, adding more weight to every over.

title: Quote of the day: ‘Be fearless and believe in your uniqueness,’ says Patrick Stewart
content: At 85, the legendary Patrick Stewart reflects on the importance of embracing fear and celebrating what makes us unique. His remarkable journey from Mirfield to the global stage, with unforgettable performances in 'Star Trek' and 'X-Men,' serves as an inspiring testament to conquering self-doubt.

title: Intel joins SpaceX Terafab project, US chipmaker CEO Lip Bu-Tan praises Elon Musk
content: Intel has partnered with Elon Musk's SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI for the "Terafab" semiconductor manufacturing project. The collaboration aims to "refactor" silicon fabrication to produce 1 Terawatt of computing power annually, crucial for future AI and robotics advancements. Intel will contribute its expertise in chip design, fabrication, and packaging to this ambitious initiative.

title: Modi’s remarks on free bus travel shows PM does not care about women's advancement: Stalin
content: Sharing the stage with VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan and DMDK general secretary Premallatha Vijayakanth to exhibit the solidarity of the alliance, Stalin said under the current DMK regime, Tamil Nadu’s growth rate jumped from a dismal 0.07% during the previous AIADMK regime to a robust 11.19%, earning the state the moniker of "Superstar State" in global media.

title: Rodgers, Steelers eye $82M move for ex-Giants All-Pro star
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers are being linked to Dexter Lawrence II following his trade request from the New York Giants, but the situation is far from straightforward. While connections with Patrick Graham and available cap space make the move plausible, Lawrence’s projected $82 million contract and recent dip in production raise valid concerns. Under new head coach Mike McCarthy, the Steelers appear to be redefining their identity, possibly leaning toward younger, cheaper talent. That makes this potential deal as risky as it is tempting.

title: Asmongold breaks silence on Twitch ban, slams double standards
content: Asmongold was banned from Twitch for a week after making a controversial remark. He defended himself online, claiming a “double standard” in enforcement. The suspension sparked mixed reactions, with some criticizing his language and others questioning Twitch’s consistency. The platform has not issued an official statement on the incident so far.

title: Chiefs linked to $83M 49ers star amid contract uncertainty
content: NFL trade rumours picked up their momentum after the Kansas City Chiefs reportedly eyed San Francisco 49ers veteran Trent Williams for their left tackle position. With ongoing contract talks between Williams and the 49ers unresolved, the Chiefs are prepared to make a trade. This move comes as Kansas City seeks long-term stability for Patrick Mahomes' blind side, despite drafting Josh Simmons last year.

title: Trump threatens to 'end civilisation'? A brief guide to life in Stone Ages
content: Since the war started, Trump has threatened to bomb Iran into the “stone age” numerous times, and unfortunately for us, and unlike the boy who cried wolf, Trump has at his disposal the greatest war machine ever assembled by the human race and with acolytes who are willing to carry out his wishes irrespective of the consequences.

title: Atmanirbhar atom: Why India’s 'fast breeder' nuclear reactor is a game-changer
content: India achieved a significant milestone as its indigenously developed 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam attained criticality. This breakthrough signals a crucial step towards full-scale power generation and enhances India's fuel self-reliance, positioning it among nations with advanced breeder reactor technology.

title: India issues advisory for nationals in Iran to stay 'where they are' for 48 hours
content: Indian embassy urged its nationals in Iran to remain indoors 'where they are' for 48 hours, avoiding specific installations, following escalating regional tensions. The advisory emphasizes staying put, especially in Embassy-hired hotels, and maintaining contact with embassy teams. This comes amid heightened warnings and reported strikes targeting key Iranian infrastructure.

title: Hardik, Boult back as MI bowl first vs RR in 11-over match - check full playing XI
content: For the Mumbai Indians, captain Hardik Pandya, who missed the last match against Delhi Capitals, and Trent Boult came back into the side.

title: Where should you put your money amid US-Iran war? 5 experts answer
content: It’s the start of a new financial year, and if you are wondering where to put your money - this article has you covered. We asked five experts what they think is the right portfolio strategy for investors at the current juncture.

title: Scientists find promising new drug against rare, life-threatening kidney disease
content: A groundbreaking oral drug, N-propargylglycine (N-PPG), has shown remarkable success in preventing fatal kidney damage in mice with Primary Hyperoxaluria Type 2 (PH2). This rare genetic disorder causes excessive oxalate buildup, leading to kidney failure. The drug effectively halts stone formation and protects kidneys, offering significant hope for a new treatment and potentially reducing the need for transplants.

title: 'Closer to Maldives': DC tag KL Rahul as Pietersen reacts to CSK mentor call; here's why
content: The exchange started on Tuesday morning when a CSK fan asked the franchise on social media to consider Pietersen for the mentor’s role.

title: US stocks today: Wall Street slides as Trump’s Iran deadline rattles markets; oil surges
content: Global markets wavered Tuesday as investors braced for President Trump's Iran deadline regarding the Strait of Hormuz. Fears of escalating Middle East conflict sent oil prices soaring and dragged down US stocks, particularly tech shares. Health insurers offered some support, but overall market sentiment remained cautious amid heightened geopolitical tensions and uncertainty.

title: 'Akele batting kar lunga': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of Mumbai Indians clash
content: Young batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, known for his aggressive 'see the ball and tonk the ball' approach, is set to face Jasprit Bumrah as Rajasthan Royals prepare to play Mumbai Indians. The 15-year-old has carried his impressive U19 World Cup form into the current season, showcasing remarkable strike rates and averages.

title: World Health Day: AI doctors vs real doctors — where machines win and where they fail
content: AI tools demonstrate impressive accuracy in medical queries but struggle with context, as seen in a doctor's test. While AI excels at speed and data analysis, human doctors remain crucial for empathy, judgment, and building trust. The future of healthcare lies in a collaborative approach, leveraging AI to enhance, not replace, human medical expertise

title: Kerala's Ezhava equation: Is the Left losing its loyal Hindu base?
content: Long before electoral arithmetic began to matter, the Ezhavas were fighting for something more fundamental. Recognition, dignity and access. In 1896, a petition signed by 13,176 members of the community was submitted to the Travancore maharaja, protesting their exclusion from government jobs. It was not merely a grievance letter. It was an early articulation of collective political consciousness.

title: Amid PSL ball-tampering ban, Fakhar Zaman denies retirement, USA move talk
content: Fakhar Zaman has quashed retirement rumours, denying any plans to move to the USA or step away from T20 internationals. Despite a recent ban and fitness concerns, sources close to the cricketer confirm his commitment to representing Pakistan, with aspirations for the 2028 T20 World Cup and the Los Angeles Olympics.

title: India EV sales hit record 24.5 lakh in FY26; cars jump 84%, 2- and 3-wheelers lead volumes
content: India's electric vehicle sales surged past 20 lakh units for the first time, driven by robust two- and three-wheeler demand. Electric cars also saw remarkable 84% growth. This milestone signifies a significant leap in the nation's green mobility transition, with all vehicle categories contributing to this impressive expansion.

title: Who is Menaka Guruswamy? SC lawyer behind Sec 377 verdict now India’s 1st openly queer MP
content: Senior advocate Menaka Guruswamy, a prominent lawyer in the Section 377 decriminalization case, has taken oath as a Trinamool Congress MP from West Bengal. She becomes India's first openly queer member of Parliament, vowing to uphold constitutional values of equality and non-discrimination.

title: India unlikely to revise fiscal deficit target despite Gulf crisis, capex stays priority
content: India remains committed to its fiscal deficit target for 2026-27, prioritizing capital expenditure despite Middle East tensions impacting oil prices. While austerity measures are considered, crucial infrastructure projects are protected. Officials suggest subsidy targeting and savings could offset added fiscal pressure, but economists warn of potential slippage if fuel prices remain capped.

title: AI images of Sushant Singh Rajput as Ram leaves sister Shweta Singh Kirti emotional
content: AI-generated images reimagining late actor Sushant Singh Rajput as Lord Ram have gone viral on social media, striking an emotional chord with his sister, Shweta Singh Kirti. Sharing the images on Instagram, Shweta opened up about why the visuals felt so powerful and deeply moving.

title: J&K: Police bust deep-rooted LeT module; arrest Pak terrorist wanted for 16 years
content: Jammu and Kashmir Police have busted a major Lashkar-e-Taiba module, arresting five individuals including a Pakistani terrorist wanted for 16 years. The operation, spanning multiple states, recovered significant weaponry and uncovered the terror group's funding network. Investigations are ongoing, with more arrests anticipated as the deep-rooted interstate network is dismantled.

title: Evacuating the ‘Dudes’: How US pulled off a high-risk rescue of F-15 pilots in Iran
content: A daring two-day US Air Force mission unfolded over Iran after an F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down. Hundreds of personnel and nearly 176 aircraft executed a complex combat search and rescue, braving heavy fire to extract both stranded airmen. Deception tactics were crucial in locating the second crew member amidst a massive Iranian manhunt.

title: SIT: Self-styled godman Ashok Kharat made ‘divine power’ claims to sexually exploit women
content: A self-styled godman, Ashok Kharat, has been arrested for allegedly exploiting women using claims of divine power and threats of death or defamation. Investigators revealed he used superstition and rituals to extort money and sexually assault victims.

title: Drake Maye’s New England Patriots strongly linked to $81m New York Giants nose tackle
content: New York Giants nose tackle Dexter Lawrence has requested a trade. He will not be participating in offseason programs as well. Bleacher Report suggests Lawrence be traded to the New England Patriots. The Giants could receive multiple draft picks in return for the proven defensive lineman. This move signals the Patriots’ aggressive approach to building a strong defense, something they did in 2025 offseason. They aimed for a Super Bowl appearance, they may continue with that approach.

title: 'Standards should not be lowered': How Tharoor reacted to Kharge's Gujarat remark
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge's remarks about Kerala voters being "educated and clever" and unlike those in Gujarat have drawn sharp criticism from the BJP. Thiruvananthapuram MP Shashi Tharoor responded by urging a higher standard of political discourse, stating that "our standards should not be lowered to that of those who use bad language."

title: Masters 2026: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .
